nodes.title: Recommended settings
widgets_values:
### Krea2 turbo
---
**sampler_name:** linear/euler
- eta: 0
- scheduler: simple
- steps: 8
- cfg: 1

**sampler_name:** exponential/res_2s
- eta: 0.7
- scheduler: bong_tangent
- steps: 8
- cfg: 1

nodes.title: FORMATED PROMPT
widgets_values: A young Caucasian woman, 20-25 years old, standing in a full-body medium shot centered in front of a sleek dark gray Audi sedan, wearing a black hoodie with the text “CUTIE AT WORK” and a colorful sugar skull graphic, paired with heavily ripped light blue denim jeans and black-and-white Vans sneakers, her short reddish-brown hair styled with bangs, wearing round dark sunglasses, adorned with multiple stacked bracelets on both wrists, hands resting flat on the car’s hood, posture upright and confident, gaze directly at the camera with a neutral expression, surrounded by blurred pedestrians on a palm-lined urban street at golden hour sunset, shallow depth of field with bokeh background, cinematic lighting with warm amber tones and high contrast shadows, photorealistic style, sharp focus on subject, natural lens compression, realistic textures and reflections, vibrant yet saturated color grading with dominant warm oranges and deep grays.

nodes.title: 💡 Tip
widgets_values: You can safely delete this "CivitAI metadata" group if you are not gonna use it. Just don't forget to enable the standard save image node.

nodes.title: 💡 Tip
widgets_values:
Change attention mode to the option compatible with your setup.
If you are in windows and you don't have sage attention installed, use SDPA.

nodes.title: 💡 Tip
widgets_values:
A seed value of -1 = Random seed
Any other number (including 0) will be treated as a fixed seed.
"Use last queued seed" will fix the seed from the last execution, same as easyGlobalSeed.
You can delete this node if you want, it sends a notification via a system notification but it has to be setup

nodes.title: MODEL LINKS
widgets_values:
**diffusion_models**

- [krea2_turbo_fp8_scaled.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/resolve/main/diffusion_models/krea2_turbo_fp8_scaled.safetensors?download=true)

**text_encoders**
- [qwen3vl-4b-abliterated_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors](https://civitai.red/models/2731465/qwen3-vl-4b-abliterated-comfyui-krea-2-text-encoder-bf16-fp8) (download the FP8 variant)

**vae**

- [qwen_image_vae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors)

**Recommended LoRAs**

- [Krea2 NSFW v3](https://civitai.red/models/2725430/krea-2-nsfw-v3?modelVersionId=3071760)

## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │   └── krea2_turbo_fp8_scaled.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │   └── qwen3vl-4b-abliterated_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │   └── qwen_image_vae.safetensors
│   └── 📂 loras/
│       └── LORAS GO HERE
```

nodes.title: Instructions
widgets_values:
**Press `0` to center the workflow on these instructions any time you need.**

⚠️ **WARNING:** Always make a backup of your ComfyUI installation before updating or installing new custom nodes so you can roll back if anything goes wrong.

---

## Prerequisites

- ComfyUI-Krea2T-Enhancer Nightly version, to access the latest patch. The node used to have a cache bug that caused the workflow to re-run the entire pipeline even when the seed was exactly the same, it was fixed in the latest version.

---

## General Instructions

> **Node color legend**
> | Color | Meaning |
> |-------|---------|
> 🔴 Red | Instructional notes — read-only reference |
> 🟡 Yellow | Configurable elements — adjust to your needs |
> 🟣 Purple | Toggle switches — enable or disable option groups |

**TIP:** Disable all groups at the start using the fast group bypasser switches (press `1` to center the workflow). Then enable groups one by one as needed. Try not to manually bypass anything; use the fast group bypasser switches instead. Otherwise, you could accidentally bypass something important. As a general rule of thumb, if it doesn't have a bypasser switch, it shouldn't be bypassed.

You can first quickly generate a set of several images at 720p queuing several generations with a random seed in one go, then pick and choose the one you like from the results, fix the seed and second pass it or upscale it.

---

## 1. Main Settings (press `1`)

### Resolution

Two resolution modes are available. Use the **Fast Groups Bypasser (rgthree)** node to switch between them:

- **Advanced image size** — use this if you are familiar with Resolution Master and want fine-grained control.
- **Simple video size** — recommended for most users; provides straightforward resolution and image orientation selection.

### LoRAs

LoRAs are loaded via the **Power LoRA Loader** node. For each LoRA you wish to use:

1. Select the LoRA file from the dropdown.
2. Adjust the **strength** value to control its influence on the generation.

> Multiple LoRAs can be stacked within the same Power LoRA Loader node.

If you are going to generate NSFW content, I strongly recommend using the [Krea2 NSFW v3](https://civitai.red/models/2725430/krea-2-nsfw-v3?modelVersionId=3071760) LoRA by [19doorsside884](https://civitai.red/user/19doorsside884).

This workflow already patches the model to remove its built-in censorship. However, some prompts may still require a little extra "punch." This LoRA gives the model the additional push it needs to fully "understand" those types of prompts.

**Recommended strength:** `1.5`

---

## 2. Prompt (press `2`)

Three prompt input methods are available. Select one based on your use case:

### Option A — Manual Prompt (default)

Enter your prompt directly into the **`POSITIVE PROMPT`** node inside the **Prompt** group. This is the standard input method. Optionally, you can enable the **`Prompt enhancer`** to allow the text encoder model to improve your base positive prompt.

### Option B — Image to Prompt (automatic)

Enable the **Image to prompt** group (purple toggle) to automatically generate a prompt from a reference image:

1. Load your reference image into the **`Reference image`** node within this group.
2. You can guide the generation by briefly describing your image in the **`POSITIVE PROMPT`** node, adding anything you want to be different from the reference image, or including details that the prompt processor might otherwise miss. In other words, you can combine the positive prompt and the reference image for greater control. In Image to Prompt mode, the positive prompt is completely optional, but I strongly recommend adding a brief description of the reference image to help the model better understand it.
3. Optionally, adjust the **seed** of the **`Prompt processor`** subgraph to explore different prompt variations.
4. To preview a generated prompt before committing to a full run, **partially execute the workflow up to the `Final prompt` node**.
5. Once satisfied, run the full workflow.

> **Tip:** To continue iterating after generation, copy the generated prompt, disable the **Image to prompt** group, paste the prompt into the **Manual prompt** group, and edit it as needed.

---

## 3. Sampling Settings *(optional)* (press `3`)

The default values for this group are production-ready and do not require adjustment for typical use. The following parameters can be modified if needed:

- **eta** — the calculated amount of noise added and then removed after each step. In practice, this works like a "smoothness" control.
- **CFG scale** — controls how strictly the model follows the prompt.
- **Sampling steps** — number of denoising iterations.
- **Sampler / Scheduler** — algorithm used for the diffusion process.

You can change the seed using the **EasyGlobalSeed** node.

---

## 4. Second Pass (press `4`)

Enable this group to perform a second pass over the main sampler generation, adding extra detail. You can control the strength of this pass by adjusting the **denoise** value.

---

## 5. Optional Groups (press `5`)

The following groups are disabled by default and can be enabled via their respective purple toggle switches.

### SeedVR2 Upscaler

Performs post-generation upscaling using **SeedVR2 Upscaler**. You can set the target resolution, select the attention mode, and choose the SeedVR2 model variant.

### Color Correction

Applies basic post-processing adjustments to the final image:

- **Brightness**
- **Contrast**
- **Saturation**

Enable this group if the generated output requires tonal correction before saving.

### Noise

Applies noise to the image.

### Sharpen

Applies basic sharpening to the image. Use the **iterations** value to control the strength (1 to 3).

---

## 6. CivitAI Metadata (press `6`)

This group automatically embeds **CivitAI-compatible metadata** into the saved output image, including generation parameters such as the prompt, seed, sampler, and model information (including LoRAs). By default, it saves the CivitAI-compatible image to your output directory under **`CivitAI`**.

- The workflow functions correctly with or without this group enabled.
- If you do not intend to upload outputs to CivitAI, this group can be safely **deleted** without affecting generation quality or workflow behavior.

nodes.title: POSITIVE PROMPT
widgets_values: A young Caucasian woman, 20-25 years old, standing in a full-body medium shot centered in front of a sleek dark gray Audi sedan, wearing a black hoodie with the text “CUTIE AT WORK” and a colorful sugar skull graphic, paired with heavily ripped light blue denim jeans and black-and-white Vans sneakers, her short reddish-brown hair styled with bangs, wearing round dark sunglasses, adorned with multiple stacked bracelets on both wrists, hands resting flat on the car’s hood, posture upright and confident, gaze directly at the camera with a neutral expression, surrounded by blurred pedestrians on a palm-lined urban street at golden hour sunset, shallow depth of field with bokeh background, cinematic lighting with warm amber tones and high contrast shadows, photorealistic style, sharp focus on subject, natural lens compression, realistic textures and reflections, vibrant yet saturated color grading with dominant warm oranges and deep grays.

nodes.title: System prompt
widgets_values:
You are an image prompt engineer. Your task is to produce a single, optimized image generation prompt.

You may receive:
- An image (analyze its style, composition, subject, lighting, and mood)
- A base prompt from the user (use it as the creative direction)
- Both (blend the visual analysis with the user's intent)
- Neither (generate a vivid, creative prompt on your own)


Rules:
- Do NOT describe the image conversationally.
- Output ONLY a prompt suitable for an image generation model.
- Avoid extra sentences like "Here is..."
- Include NSFW descriptins if aplicable.

Prompt requirements:
1. Subject description:
   - Identify and explicitly state the ethnicity and age immediately
   - Body proportions (highly important)
   - Facial features, skin texture, expression, gaze direction.
   - Hair style, hair color, accessories, jewlery, etc.
   - Clothing, materials, fit, layers

2. Pose and composition:
   - Body pose, hand position, posture.
   - Framing (close-up, medium shot, full body)
   - Camera angle (eye level, front view, back view, low angle, top-down, etc)
   - Subject placement in frame
   - Character Dynamics: If characters are present in the image or text, explicitly describe their physical actions, spatial relationship, and interactions with one another. Describe any sexual intercourse if any.

3. Environment and background:
   - Location type (studio, indoor, outdoor)
   - Background color, texture, objects
   - Depth of field

4. Lighting:
   - Light direction, softness, contrast
   - Time of day or artificial lighting style

5. Artistic and technical style:
   - Photorealistic, cinematic, illustration, anime, 3D render, etc.
   - Lens look (wide, portrait compression), bokeh if visible
   - Image sharpness, noise, realism level

6. Color and mood:
   - Dominant colors
   - Color grading (warm, cool, neutral, muted, vibrant)

Formatting rules:
- Output as a single, highly descriptive paragraph of natural, dense prose.
- Use concrete, reproducible descriptors.

Base prompt:
Krea2 [SFW / NSFW] Uncensored - Image-to-Prompt + Prompt Enhancer + 4K Upscaler + CivitAI Metadata
